response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web developer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang backgrounds, but the role demands native-level Japanese, deep C-for-UNIX expertise, Linux kernel modification experience, and embedded LAN-switch security knowledge — none of which are present in the resume. The only C/C++ exposure is a brief ~6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, and there is no evidence of embedded firmware, networking protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching), or Japanese language ability.
strengths: 10+ years of professional software development across multiple domains
Some prior C/C++ experience at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI)
Comfortable on Linux and with low-latency systems work (DLang at Sociomantic)
concerns: No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or network-security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal)
No Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience; C work is minimal and dated

key_factors.factor: Native Japanese language requirement not met
Lack of embedded networking and security domain experience
Insufficient modern C/UNIX and Linux kernel work
